title: Resize landing page logo and add YouTube link to CMS

requirements.text: On the landing page (/), reduce the game logo (gamelogo.png) size so it appears noticeably smaller than its current size on desktop. It should still be responsive (scaling down on mobile/tablet with max-width: 100% and auto height), but the desktop maximum size must be reduced — use a max-width in the range of 480px–560px (down from the current full natural width) so it no longer dominates the viewport.

quotes: the Logo is now too big make it a bit smaller

acceptanceCriteria: On desktop, the game logo is visibly smaller than in the previous version and does not dominate the viewport
The logo scales down fluidly on mobile and tablet without cropping or overflow
max-width: 100% and auto height are still applied so the logo never breaks the layout on any screen size
Dark mode inversion (CSS filter invert) is preserved on the logo

requirements.text: Add a stable text variable `youtubeLink` to the backend actor in backend/main.mo, initialized to 'https://youtu.be/5in-hIASH08'. Include it in the record returned by getContent(). Add an admin-only update method updateYoutubeLink(link: Text) that verifies the caller is the registered admin before persisting the new value. Reject with 'Access denied' otherwise.

quotes: add an entry for the youtube link
make it so the admin can edit the youtube video link in the cms

acceptanceCriteria: A stable variable `youtubeLink: Text` exists in main.mo and persists across upgrades
updateYoutubeLink(link: Text) is an admin-only update method that rejects non-admin callers with 'Access denied'
The default value 'https://youtu.be/5in-hIASH08' is set so existing behaviour is unchanged before any admin edit

requirements.text: Add a YouTube Link field to the Admin Dashboard (/admin) CMS. It must be a text input pre-populated with the current youtubeLink value from getContent(), with a Save button that calls updateYoutubeLink() and then invalidates/refetches the content query. Add the corresponding useUpdateYoutubeLink mutation to frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts.

quotes: add in the CMS system and add an entry for the youtube link
make it so the admin can edit the youtube video link in the cms

acceptanceCriteria: The Admin Dashboard shows a clearly labelled 'YouTube Link' text input field in the CMS section
The input is pre-populated with the current youtubeLink value from the backend
Clicking Save calls updateYoutubeLink() with the new value
After a successful save, the content query is invalidated and refetched so the press kit reflects the updated link immediately
A success or error state is shown to the admin after saving

requirements.text: Update the VideoSection component on /press-kit to use the youtubeLink value from the global content query (returned by getContent()) instead of a hardcoded YouTube URL. When the user clicks the video thumbnail play button, the embedded YouTube iframe must use this admin-editable link.

quotes: currently we have a youtube video in the presskit but make it so the admin can edit the youtube video link in the cms

acceptanceCriteria: The VideoSection component receives the youtubeLink from the CMS content query rather than a hardcoded string
Clicking the video thumbnail replaces it with a YouTube iframe using the admin-set youtubeLink
If youtubeLink is empty or not yet set, the component falls back gracefully (e.g., the play button does nothing or is hidden)

constraints: Do not modify any file under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
All backend logic must remain in the single actor in backend/main.mo
The youtubeLink default value must be 'https://youtu.be/5in-hIASH08' to preserve existing press kit behaviour
nonGoals: Changing any other CMS fields or backend methods beyond youtubeLink
Modifying the press kit page layout beyond wiring up the dynamic YouTube link
Changing the logo size on the press kit page — only the landing page logo is being resized
Adding YouTube video preview or validation logic
